title: V51.1 Refraction Cascade: How One Parameter Fix Breaks Everything

human_decision.structural_placement: New section under The Model tab (option a), NOT buried in Section 7.10/7.11 kill shots. The cascade is load-bearing enough that many affected WIN detail panels should cross-reference it as their primary self-contradiction argument, so it needs its own real estate where dome geometry is first introduced.

new_code_analysis_tag.nature: sub-category tag, not a new top-level verdict
new_code_analysis_tag.analogous_to: Existing code_analysis tags like relabels_standard, post_hoc, derives_from_dome — boolean structural markers
new_code_analysis_tag.proposed_name_working: flipped_by_refraction_cascade (analyst should propose better naming options)
new_code_analysis_tag.semantics: TRUE if this WIN's verdict is affected or flipped by the V51.1 physical/apparent sun altitude split and its cascading refraction requirements. Used to mark which WINs cross-reference the new section as their primary or secondary self-contradiction basis.
new_code_analysis_tag.schema_impact: Adds one boolean field to wins.json schema under code_analysis. Needs a count placeholder for the summary prose (e.g. {{CA_CASCADE_FLIPPED}}) analogous to existing CA_RELABELS etc. Curmudgeon should validate the name and semantics before implementation.

human_decision.process_order: Analyst writes a RECOMMENDED PROPOSAL PACKAGE (not section prose yet). Package routed to curmudgeon for review. Only after curmudgeon signs off does anyone flesh out actual section content. This is to lock in the structural decisions before token spend on prose.
human_decision.alternatives_to_retain_in_package: Options (b) per-WIN patches instead of new section, and (c) extension of Section 7.10/7.11 kill-shot tab, must both be documented in the proposal so curmudgeon can weigh them against option (a). Do not delete or hide them.

rationale: The V51.1 physical/apparent sun altitude split creates a single coherent cascade failure across 12+ WINs. This is better presented as a unified cross-cutting argument than as scattered notes in individual WIN rebuttals. The argument has quantitative backbone: H(r) ceiling at sun orbit = 1,975-3,618 km (not 4,200), refraction needed = 13-27° (vs 0.03° real), n(r) inconsistency = 400-675x, seasonal angular diameter variation = 83%, tidal force modulation = 3-8x.
recommended_location: New subsection in Part 1 (The Model) introducing the V51.1 split as a self-contradiction BEFORE readers reach individual WIN rebuttals. Alternative locations (Part 2 Self-Contradictions, or Section 7.10/7.11 extension) retained for curmudgeon review.
outline: 1. The split: physical <4,200 km -> actually <1,975 km (ceiling at winter solstice orbit)
2. Refraction needed: 13-27 degrees varying seasonally (vs 0.03 degrees real atmospheric)
3. The n(r) contradiction: dome own formula gives ~0.04 degrees for stars, not 15+ degrees for the sun
4. Universal displacement: moon, stars, all triangulation baselines affected
5. Seasonal modulation: 83% angular diameter variation, 3-8x tidal force variation
6. Eclipse geometry paradox: shadows use physical positions, but physical altitude undefined
7. Self-defeating fix: the split was meant to resolve collision but creates model-wide inconsistency

proposed_verdict_flips.note: Analyst to propose which WINs should flip to Self-Contradicted once the cascade section lands. Curmudgeon to validate each flip. Working hypothesis: 7 WINs plausibly flip (010, 016, 017, 018, 019, 026, 044, 068) with 065 staying Refuted by Data. All flipped WIN detail panels should retain their existing data rebuttal as secondary evidence — primary argument becomes self-contradiction, data rebuttal cited as 'also'.
